title: Air Products Wins More than $140 Million in NASA Contracts to Supply Liquid Hydrogen to the Kennedy Space Center, Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and Other NASA Facilities
content: Air Products announced that it was recently awarded supply contracts from the National Aeronautics and Space Administration (NASA) totaling more than $140 million to provide liquid hydrogen for several NASA facilities including the worldâ€™s largest hydrogen sphere at NASAâ€™s Kennedy Space Center, the Cape Canaveral Space Force Station and other NASA locations. â€œFrom the inception of the United States (U.S.) space program, Air Products has supported NASAâ€™s mission by supplying the critical industrial gases needed from the initial Apollo 11 moon landing to the upcoming Artemis II moon mission,â€ said Francesco Maione, Air Productsâ€™ President, Americas. â€œFor decades, Air Products has consistently demonstrated our ability to supply world-scale levels of liquid hydrogen and other industrial gases safely and reliably through our robust supply chain. Weâ€™re proud to play a role in helping NASA confidently continue its important work.â€ Under the new contract, Air Products will supply about 36.5 million pounds of liquid hydrogen to NASA for the Kennedy Space Center and Cape Canaveral Space Force Station in Florida; NASAâ€™s Marshall Space Flight Center in Huntsville, Alabama; and NASAâ€™s Stennis Space Center in Bay St. Louis, Mississippi.
author: Analysts Account
